description: Standard trend model for a new market entry scenario with relationship matrix and scenarios
prompt: I'm evaluating entering the electric vehicle charging station market. Can you build a trend model? The key variables I care about are: EV adoption rate, charging infrastructure density, electricity costs, and government subsidies. I don't have hard numbers, just directional intuition.

expectations.description: Output contains a Variables table with all four user-specified variables and their trend assignments (INC/DEC/CONST)
Output contains a Relationship Matrix showing INC/DEC/CONST correlations between variable pairs
Output generates at least two named scenarios (e.g., S1, S2) with trend assignments for each variable
Output identifies at least one terminal scenario and explains why it is an equilibrium state
Output includes a Mermaid state diagram showing transitions between scenarios

description: Tests extended notation (AG/DG/AD/DD) for nuanced trend analysis
prompt: I need a trend model for the remote work technology market. Use the extended notation with acceleration/deceleration — I want to see AG, DG, AD, DD where appropriate, not just plain INC/DEC. Variables: remote workforce adoption, office real estate demand, collaboration tool spend, cybersecurity investment.

expectations.description: Output uses extended notation codes AG, DG, AD, or DD in scenario assignments
Output explains what the extended notation codes mean (accelerating/decelerating growth/decrease)
Output contains a relationship matrix linking variable pairs
Output addresses all four user-specified variables in the model
Output generates scenarios using the extended notation rather than only basic INC/DEC/CONST

description: Tests explicit correlation-to-trend conversion with positive and negative correlations
prompt: I have these known correlations for the renewable energy sector: solar panel cost and adoption rate are negatively correlated, government incentives and adoption rate are positively correlated, adoption rate and grid strain are positively correlated, and grid strain and electricity price are positively correlated. Convert these into a trend model with INC/DEC notation and show me the scenarios.

expectations.description: Output correctly converts negative correlation: when solar panel cost is DEC, adoption is INC (or vice versa)
Output correctly converts positive correlation: when government incentives is INC, adoption is INC
Output uses the INC/DEC notation format described in the skill (e.g., INC(X, Y) or DEC(X, Y))
Output produces a relationship matrix covering all four variables
Output generates at least two consistent scenarios that satisfy all relationship constraints

description: Tests multi-objective trade-off analysis across terminal scenarios
prompt: Build me a trend model for a food delivery startup. Variables: delivery speed, driver compensation, order volume, customer satisfaction, profit margins. I need to understand the trade-offs — which terminal scenarios favor profitability vs. growth vs. driver welfare? Show me why I can't have everything.

expectations.description: Output explicitly identifies competing objectives and maps terminal scenarios to objectives
Output includes terminal scenario analysis with at least two terminal scenarios
Output addresses all three requested objectives: profitability, growth, and driver welfare
Output explains why no single scenario satisfies all objectives simultaneously
Output includes a Mermaid transitional scenario graph
Output provides strategic recommendations based on priority alignment

description: Edge case: only two variables provided, testing model behavior at minimal complexity
prompt: quick trend model: just two variables — interest rates and housing demand. what scenarios come out?

expectations.description: Output identifies the negative correlation between interest rates and housing demand
Output still produces a structured model with relationship matrix despite only two variables
Output generates scenarios using INC/DEC/CONST assignments for both variables
Output does NOT contain placeholder text or unfilled template markers

description: Edge case: user asks for a trend model without specifying variables, testing skill's ability to propose them
prompt: can you do a three-valued logic analysis for the fintech lending space? I don't really know what variables matter, just help me think through it

expectations.description: Output proposes a set of relevant variables (at least 4) for the fintech lending domain
Output follows the standard output structure with Variables table, Relationship Matrix, and Scenarios
Output includes confidence levels (High/Med/Low) reflecting the speculative nature of proposed variables
Output documents uncertainty or notes where relationships are speculative, per best practices
Output includes a transitional scenario graph in Mermaid format

description: Edge case: user provides 7+ variables to test handling of complex relationship matrices
prompt: I need a full trend model for the healthcare AI market with these variables: regulatory approval speed, data privacy requirements, clinician adoption rate, patient trust, AI model accuracy, reimbursement policies, competing solutions count. That's a lot of relationships to map — give me the full matrix and the key scenarios.

expectations.description: Output includes a relationship matrix that covers all 7 specified variables
Output relationship matrix uses INC/DEC/CONST for variable pair relationships
Output generates scenarios and identifies which are terminal vs. transitional
Output includes Key Insights section summarizing takeaways from the complex model
Output includes a Mermaid transitional graph despite the complexity

description: NxN relationship matrix with 5 variables using INC/DEC/CONST assignments
prompt: Build a trend model for the EdTech market with variables: student enrollment, tuition costs, online platform adoption, teacher availability, and venture funding. Show the full relationship matrix.

expectations.description: Output contains a relationship matrix
Output includes INC trend assignments
Output includes DEC trend assignments
Output includes CONST trend assignments
NxN matrix where N=5 variables, all pairs have INC/DEC/CONST assignments

description: Multi-objective trade-off where no single scenario optimizes all objectives
prompt: Model the trade-offs in ride-sharing: driver pay, rider price, platform margin, service quality, market share. Show me the terminal scenarios and explain the trade-offs.

expectations.description: Output explicitly states that no single scenario optimizes all objectives
Output identifies terminal scenarios
Explicit statement that no single scenario optimizes all objectives simultaneously
